$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Energy repricing risk from Black Sea maritime disruption state
foil.scenario_short: Energy repricing risk from Black Sea maritime disruption state remains at 59% probability over 30d, driven by a 62% pressure and 71% state confirmation feeding gas supply stress, with 7 urgent critical signals...
foil.perspective_strategic: The 62% pressure and 71% state confirmation indicate a strong transmission path through gas supply stress, with 7 urgent critical signals reinforcing energy pressure, suggesting sustained market repricing risk.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The 75% confidence level leaves room for reversal, and the lack of more directly matched reporting on Black Sea could prevent escalation, as actors prioritize containment and the system stabilizes without new hard signals.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Black Sea becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 59%.
foil.top_signal: Black Sea maritime disruption state is feeding energy repricing through gas supply stress, with 62% pressure and 71% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
